exemplar.title: Finding the estimated cost of painting a wooden tree-shaped artifact

detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the estimated cost of painting a wooden tree-shaped artifact

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical narrative with distinct introduction, body, and conclusion
Effective use of headings, sub-headings, figures, and tables for navigation
Material is mostly relevant with high-quality modelling figures and a clear title page
weaknesses: Minor digressions in background theory disrupt flow
Table on page 5 splits across fragments, affecting readability
Some repeated descriptions reduce overall conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Presentation strands sit firmly in band 3: coherent logical development, well-organized structure, and mostly concise content. Minor layout and repetition issues prevent band 4, so a score of 3/4 accurately reflects the quality.

strengths: Clear definitions, diagram labels, and explanations make the mathematics readable
Appropriate use of symbols (Ï, integrals, RÂ²) and variable glossary
Effective multiple representations: tables, 2D plots, colour-coded sets, GeoGebra screenshots
weaknesses: Occasional notation inconsistencies (x vs Ï) and typographical slips
Mismatched braces and plain-text exponents in places
Representations are effective but not fully integrated at a sophisticated level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently demonstrates clear communication and appropriate notation (band 3), with effective multiple representations. Minor formatting inconsistencies and the absence of a higher level of integration prevent a band 4, yielding 3/4.

strengths: Strong personal business context driving the investigation
Independent initiative in choosing bespoke piece-wise models
Creative use of curve families and verification methods
weaknesses: Methodology relies on standard GeoGebra and CAS procedures
Personal approach, while clear, stops short of deep mathematical insight
Initiative is significant rather than outstanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and creativity consistent with band 2. It lacks the level of originality and depth required for band 3, so 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement uncertainty, modelling assumptions, and business implications
Clear critique of RÂ² values and bounding checks
Links between mathematics results and pricing decisions are well articulated
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive without deep quantitative error analysis
Critical analysis, while present, lacks the depth of substantial critique
Connections to enhanced mathematical understanding are stated rather than fully evidenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands for depth, critical analysis, and connection to understanding align with band 2: meaningful reflection and critique are evident but not sufficiently deep for band 3. Thus, a 2/3 score is justified.

strengths: Use of HL-level surface-of-revolution integrals and piece-wise fitting is appropriate
Calculations are correct with only minor rounding uncertainties
Solid understanding of formulas and rationale for model choices
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies and CAS reliance introduce small uncertainties
No rigorous error-propagation or theoretical derivations
Simplification as a solid of revolution may under-represent surface irregularities
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics used is relevant and effective at course level with mostly correct accuracy and good understanding (band 4). The absence of sophisticated error analysis or rigorous derivations prevents a higher band, making 4/6 the fitting score.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personal business problem and deciding to create a bespoke piece-wise model shows significant independent thinking; the student does more than replicate textbook methods.  Nevertheless, much of the technique (GeoGebra Fit commands, direct CAS integration) follows standard procedures, so the evidence is significant rather than outstanding.

The student explains the choice of modelling functions, interprets RÂ², and shows solid grasp of surface area formulae, indicating good understanding throughout.  Absence of deeper theoretical justification (e.g., derivation of surface formula, statistical error bounds) prevents classification as âthorough with rigourâ.

Derivatives, integrals and unit calculations appear correct; bounding-shape verification supports accuracy.  Minor rounding inconsistencies and reliance on CAS for lengthy integrals introduce small uncertainties, so the work fits âcorrect with minor errorsâ rather than the flawless precision required for higher bands.

Nearly all material included is relevant to estimating the painting cost.  However, several explanatory paragraphs (extended quotations from sources, very detailed glossary) could have been condensed.  Hence it is ârelevant and mostly concise,â not uniformly concise throughout.

Correct symbols (Ï, integral notation, RÂ², piece-wise definition) are used consistently, and variables are defined in a glossary.  A few minor formatting slips (e.g., mismatched braces, occasional plain-text exponents) prevent a fully âconsistent and accurateâ band 4.

Explanations, variable definitions and diagram labels make the mathematics readable.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., switching between x and Ï, small typographical errors) appear, but overall clarity is maintained, matching a âclear and mostly consistentâ description.

Surface-of-revolution integrals, piece-wise curve fitting, use of RÂ² and unit conversions are all at HL level and are applied purposefully to the context, representing mathematics used effectively at the course level.  The work is not mathematically sophisticated enough (e.g., no analytic derivations or rigorous error analysis) for bands 5-6.

The student employs tables of measurements, numerous 2-D plots, colour-coded point sets, GeoGebra screenshots and 3-D renderings of surfaces of revolution.  These are appropriate and clearly linked to the text, demonstrating effective multiple representations.  The work stops short of the âsophisticatedâ integration of representations expected for band 4.

The student reflects on measurement uncertainty, modelling assumptions, paint absorption and profit-margin choices, indicating meaningful reflection throughout the report.  Reflection is largely descriptive and does not consistently probe deeper consequences, so it is not âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Using piece-wise functions, experimenting with different curve families and verifying the area by bounding shapes demonstrates notable creativity and initiative.  However, the methods remain within well-known GeoGebra and calculus techniques, so the work does not reach the âoutstandingâ descriptor.

The report follows a clear narrative: introduction, data collection, modelling, calculations, verification, reflection and conclusion.  Transitions between these sections are logical, and each step is justified, showing the reader how the investigation develops.  Minor digressions (e.g., long explanations of basic theory already familiar to the HL audience) slightly interrupt flow, so the development is clear rather than âexcellent.â

There is analysis of model fitness via RÂ², a bounding-surface validity check, and discussion of business implications.  This offers clear critique but lacks depth (e.g., no quantitative error propagation), positioning it in the âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ band.

The student links mathematical results to business pricing decisions and sustainability aims, demonstrating a clear connection between mathematics and personal understanding.  The enhancement to mathematical understanding is stated rather than deeply evidenced, keeping it within band 2.

Headings, sub-headings, figures and tables are used effectively; the reader can locate information quickly.  Introduction, body and conclusion are distinct and in a logical order.  The structure is solid but not âexceptionally seamlessâ because some description is duplicated in several places (e.g., repeated mention of RÂ² and paint-cost motivation).

The narrative is clearly driven by the studentâs own contextâpricing for a real customerâand decisions such as measurement strategy and model selection are justified from that perspective.  This constitutes a âclear personal approachâ, but not one that fundamentally enhances mathematical insight to the degree required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The plan to use a piecewise function is appropriate for capturing complex geometry; explicitly list each subdomain and its modelling rationale.
Color-coded representation of different curve segments enhances clarity in multiple representations.
A perfect RÂ²=1 for the first linear segment may signal overfitting or insufficient data points. Discuss this to demonstrate deeper critical analysis.
When introducing each piecewise equation, state its exact domain interval in mathematical notation to improve clarity and consistency.
In the quadratic model description, specify why a downward parabola fits the blue points, referencing point coordinates and curvature.
The use of Ï instead of x in notation can cause confusion. Standardize on one variable symbol and define it clearly in the glossary.
Noting the small deviation at point B is good; quantifying this error in mm or percentage would strengthen the analysis.
The title page effectively communicates the subject and focus of the exploration, providing clear context for the investigation ahead.
The concise project title clearly states the research question, aiding reader orientation. Consider adding a brief subtitle to highlight methodology.
The personal narrative in the introduction engagingly connects the studentâs business context to the mathematical aim, demonstrating clear personal engagement.
The extended quotations and glossary material interrupt flow. Condense or integrate key definitions only to maintain conciseness and relevance.
The sentence ending with âAreaâ is cut off, leaving the concept incomplete. Please clarify which formula or definition was intended here.
Modeling the handcrafted artifact as a solid of revolution simplifies the problem but may under-represent irregular surface features. Consider quantifying this approximationâs impact.
Specifying millimeters to two decimal places shows careful mathematical communication and consideration of measurement accuracy.
Sketching the intended model before data collection helps visualize which measurements are necessary. Consider including this sketch earlier for alignment.
Reflection on the string-slippage issue shows thoughtful critical analysis of measurement methods.
The formula $$r=\frac{c}{2\pi}$$ is correctly applied and clearly presented, showing solid understanding of circle geometry.
Although the table of measurements is clear, include column labels with units in the caption to enhance presentation consistency.
Page 5âs table splits across two fragments, affecting readability. Consolidate the table or adjust layout to avoid breaks.
Describing the GeoGebra scaling process demonstrates sound mathematical communication; include a brief workflow summary to guide replication.
The inclusion of high-quality figures for modelling is effective; ensure all figures have descriptive captions and references in the text.
Choosing the Fit command in GeoGebra shows effective use of technology to reduce manual error, reflecting sound use of mathematics.
RÂ² is introduced conceptually, but you should display its formula and explain how itâs computed to improve mathematical communication.
Rounding coefficients to six significant figures improves precision, but consider performing a brief error analysis to justify this choice.
The rationale for discarding the sine model despite its high RÂ² demonstrates critical evaluation of mathematical suitability.
When fitting different functions, include a table comparing RÂ², coefficient complexity, and domain to clarify model selection.
Identifying five turning points and opting for a 5th-degree polynomial shows independent initiative in modelling complexity.
